message:
fix(node): enforce temporal ordering in token delegation chains and correct nonce replay tracking

Two targeted fixes to the audience token validation subsystem:

1. Add temporal ordering enforcement for delegated tokens: child
   tokens must not have an issued_at timestamp earlier than their
   parent token. This prevents backdated delegation attacks where an
   attacker could create a token appearing to predate a revocation
   event. Violations now return ERR_ABT_ATTENUATION_VIOLATION with
   a descriptive message including both timestamps.

2. Revert nonce tracking to record only the leaf token's nonce
   rather than all token nonces in the chain. The previous change
   (recording all nonces) was incorrect because intermediate token
   nonces are structurally determined by the chain construction and
   don't need replay protection — only the leaf nonce (which the
   bearer actually presents) needs deduplication. Recording all
   nonces would incorrectly reject valid chains that legitimately
   reuse intermediate delegation tokens across multiple leaf tokens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
